EXP-190 (draft_awaiting_human_group_review) and EXP-435 (pending) are the other live items — both non-terminal, correct. ID gaps (43 IDs missing in range 1-435) are inherited from prior sessions — not new, consistent with previous runs, and not flagged in prior reports. No new gaps introduced.

WIN-070 covers dome home page forensic exhibit, not a separately-numbered WIN — previously closed as ISS-2205 (wontfix). No new mismatches. review-response.html still reachable at john09289.github.io/predictions/review-response.html.

mech_a_bypass_audit.details: 3 PROP-059 violation candidates found post-cutoff (2026-05-25T15:00Z) without verification_pattern and without audit_grandfathered: (1) ISS-2202 second close by decider-step1d-same-run-self-apply at 2026-05-26T14:37 — EXP tracker rename fix; (2) ISS-2203 second close by decider-step1d-same-run-self-apply at 2026-05-26T14:37 — EXP tracker archive move; (3) ISS-2207 by decider-priority3-self-apply at 2026-05-26T14:39 — WIN-063 tldr_verdict text fix. Note: ISS-2207 fix IS confirmed in wins.json (asymmetric decay present, asymmetry ratio absent) and tests pass (3692/3692). ISS-2202/2203 first-close instances ARE properly grandfathered. The second-close instances represent a decider re-applying already-closed issues without the PROP-059 discipline.

issues_found.description: ISS-2202 second close (decider-step1d-same-run-self-apply, 2026-05-26T14:37) has no verification_pattern and no audit_grandfathered. First close (decider-2026-05-25T17-17) is properly grandfathered. Second close appears to be decider re-applying an already-closed issue. Fix subject: EXP-432 renamed to EXP-434 in live tracker.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Verify EXP-434 was correctly renamed/handled. If the expansion tracker change was legitimate, add audit_grandfathered: 'decider-step1d-double-close-2026-05-26' to the second close entry. If the re-close was spurious, operator removes the second entry.

issues_found.description: ISS-2203 second close (decider-step1d-same-run-self-apply, 2026-05-26T14:37) has no verification_pattern and no audit_grandfathered. Fix subject: moved EXP-425, EXP-431 from live tracker to archive.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Verify EXP-425 and EXP-431 are correctly in archive. If legitimate, add audit_grandfathered to second close. If spurious, remove second entry.

issues_found.description: ISS-2207 closed by decider-priority3-self-apply at 2026-05-26T14:39 without verification_pattern. Closure_reason cites tests 3692/3692 pass. Fix IS confirmed: WIN-063 tldr_verdict in wins.json contains 'asymmetric decay' (not 'asymmetry ratio') as of HEAD. Fix is real but PROP-059 field discipline was not followed.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Add verification_pattern: 'grep -c "asymmetric decay" data/wins.json | grep -v "asymmetry ratio"' to ISS-2207 entry (or add audit_grandfathered: 'fix-confirmed-2026-05-28-integrity' since fix is verified). Write open ISS flagging the missing field.

issues_found.description: EXP-433 (status=complete, integrated=true) remains in the live expansion-tracker items array. Should have been moved to archive by decider integration writer. No data loss — the integration happened — but the live-state predicate (integrated !== true) is violated.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Move EXP-433 from live items[] to expansion-tracker-archive.jsonl. Decider integration writer should append to archive and remove from live atomically.

summary: Site is structurally sound. Test suite passes 3692/3692. Build reproducibility is clean (date-only diff expected). All internal anchors, tabs, data-prose consistency, and discoverability infrastructure are healthy. The drift audit was refreshed (5 candidates for curmudgeon review). Three PROP-059 Mech-A-bypass violations were detected in closed-issues.json for entries closed on 2026-05-26: ISS-2202, ISS-2203 (second closes of already-grandfathered issues by decider-step1d-same-run-self-apply), and ISS-2207 (priority3 self-apply on a tldr_verdict text fix whose result IS confirmed in wins.json). All three are missing the verification_pattern field required by PROP-059. The ISS-2207 fix is verified genuine; the ISS-2202/2203 second closes need operator review to determine if they were spurious re-applies or legitimate separate fixes. One moderate finding: EXP-433 remains in the live expansion tracker array with integrated=true and should be moved to archive. Open issues count is 0 — decider BAU triage on 2026-05-27 legitimately closed all 3 prior open issues (ISS-2204 wontfix, ISS-2205 wontfix, ISS-2206 route-to-analyst).
